error.signature: AI tells someone in Morocco to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Morocco does not use 911. In cities dial 19 for police; in rural areas the Gendarmerie Royale is 177. Ambulance/SAMU is 15 and fire (Protection Civile) is 150. 112 from a mobile also works.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Morocco expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Morocco. Police is 19 in cities and 177 (gendarmerie) in rural areas; ambulance is 15. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Dial 19 (urban police) when you are outside a city
dead_ends.why_fails: Outside urban areas the Gendarmerie Royale (177) has jurisdiction, not the 19 police line; dialing 19 in the countryside reaches the wrong service.

workarounds.how: City police: 19. Rural gendarmerie: 177. Ambulance: 15. Fire: 150. Mobile: 112.

workarounds.action: From a mobile, 112 also routes to emergency services anywhere in Morocco.
workarounds.how: Use 19 (city) or 177 (rural) for police, 15 for ambulance, 150 for fire, or 112 from a mobile.
